title: Monotone-constraint booster on physics-safe features
summary: XGBoost supports explicit monotonic constraints, which fit several pit-stop priors: more stop debt, more excess tyre age versus finish window, and larger positive degradation pressure should not reduce stop probability all else equal.
rationale: XGBoost supports explicit monotonic constraints, which fit several pit-stop priors: more stop debt, more excess tyre age versus finish window, and larger positive degradation pressure should not reduce stop probability all else equal. A constrained expert can cut fold variance from spurious inversions.
implementation_hint: Train a separate XGBoost model on a compact set of engineered signals with signed constraints, e.g. increasing for CurrentTyreFinishExcess, Positive_LapTime_Delta, Deg_Per_TyreLife, PriorStopDebt, and decreasing for viable fresh-tyre finish margin. Keep high-cardinality categoricals out of this expert and stack OOF.
expected_effect: May not raise standalone CV much, but often improves robustness and ensemble diversity, especially when hand-engineered window features already dominate.
risk: Wrong constraints can suppress real interactions, and the hist builder may need larger max_bin to avoid overly shallow trees.
